title: Mardi Gras Parade — Biloxi
content: Biloxi's Mardi Gras celebrations feature one of the largest parades on the Mississippi Gulf Coast. Krewes roll through the streets of downtown Biloxi and along the beachfront, throwing beads, doubloons, and moon pies to cheering crowds. Biloxi's Mardi Gras tradition dates back to 1908.

title: Mardi Gras Parade — Gulfport
content: Gulfport celebrates Mardi Gras with a festive parade along Highway 49 and through downtown. Local krewes, marching bands, and decorated floats fill the streets with beads, music, and celebration.

title: Pop UP-DOWNtown
content: Downtown Biloxi's monthly community gathering at City Hall Plaza with live music, food vendors, and local artisans. This free event transforms downtown Biloxi into an outdoor celebration. Themes vary monthly.

title: Ship Island Excursions — 100th Anniversary Season
content: Ship Island Excursions celebrates 100 years of the Skrmetta family operation! Board the ferry in Gulfport for a scenic ride across the Mississippi Sound to Ship Island, home of historic Fort Massachusetts. Enjoy swimming, shelling, and exploring pristine barrier island beaches. Lifeguards on duty in summer.

title: Taste of South Mississippi
content: Sample dishes from the region's top restaurants while socializing and bidding in a lively silent auction. This popular annual event benefits the Kroc Center's scholarship fund, supporting community programs across the Gulf Coast.

title: Gulf Coast Fair
content: The Gulf Coast Fair returns to the Mississippi Coast Coliseum with thrilling midway rides, delicious fair food, games, and attractions for the entire family. From classic carnival rides to live entertainment, one of the biggest annual events on the coast.

title: CLASH Endurance Coastal Mississippi Triathlon
content: CLASH Endurance brings world-class triathlon racing to Gulfport! This multi-day event features the USA Triathlon Collegiate Club National Championships alongside age group triathlons and duathlons.

title: D'Iberville BBQ Festival
content: A free, family-friendly celebration of barbecue in D'Iberville! Watch pitmasters compete in the BBQ cook-off, enjoy live music, and cast your vote for the best barbecue on the Gulf Coast.

title: Biloxi Shuckers Opening Day
content: The Biloxi Shuckers kick off their 2026 season at Keesler Federal Park! Enjoy America's pastime with Gulf Coast flair — great ballpark food, family fun zones, and exciting Double-A baseball action under the lights.

title: Gulf Coast Grand Prix
content: Formula One Powerboat Championship races roar across the Mississippi Sound in Biloxi! Between high-speed races, enjoy a Cajun street party along the beach, a boat parade, and live entertainment.

title: Crawfish Music Festival
content: Biloxi's annual Crawfish Music Festival combines the best of Gulf Coast cuisine and live music. Enjoy a crawfish cook-off, live musical performances from regional artists, cold drinks, and all the boiled crawfish you can eat.

title: Long Beach Crab Festival
content: Long Beach's signature event celebrates blue crabs with a crab-cooking competition, live music, arts and crafts, kids' activities, and plenty of fresh Gulf Coast seafood.
address: Jeff Davis Ave., Long Beach, MS 39560

title: Cinco de Mayo Fiesta — Gulfport
content: Gulfport celebrates Cinco de Mayo with a festive fiesta featuring Mexican and Latin American cuisine, live mariachi music, folklorico dance performances, and family activities.

title: CPR Fest
content: Multi-band rock festival featuring Staind, Sleep Theory, and more at the Mississippi Coast Coliseum. One of the biggest concert events of the year on the Gulf Coast.

title: Pass Christian Seafood Festival
content: Pass Christian hosts its annual Seafood Festival at War Memorial Park, showcasing the best of Gulf Coast cuisine. Fresh shrimp, oysters, crab, and fish are served up alongside live music, arts and crafts, and family activities.

title: Biloxi Shuckers Military Appreciation Night
content: The Shuckers honor all branches of the armed forces with alternate military-themed jerseys. Part of Memorial Day Weekend series. Military discounts available.

title: Jazz in the Pass
content: Live jazz music fills War Memorial Park overlooking the Mississippi Sound on Memorial Day weekend. A beloved Pass Christian tradition featuring regional jazz musicians.

title: Memorial Day Weekend Beach Party
content: Kick off summer on Biloxi Beach with a Memorial Day weekend celebration. Live music, beach volleyball tournaments, food trucks, and family activities line the beachfront.

title: South Mississippi Summer Fair
content: Over 33 years of family-friendly fun with carnival rides, midway games, live music, shows, contests, food vendors, and entertainment. One of the region's biggest summer traditions at the Mississippi Coast Coliseum.

title: Gulfport Music Festival
content: The Gulfport Music Festival showcases regional and national musical acts at Jones Park. Multiple stages feature blues, rock, country, and Gulf Coast soul. Enjoy craft beer gardens, food vendors, and a vibrant festival atmosphere on the waterfront.

title: Juneteenth Celebration — Gulfport
content: Gulfport celebrates Juneteenth with a community-wide event honoring African American history and culture. The celebration features live music, guest speakers, soul food, vendors, and activities for families.

title: 24th Annual Scrapin' the Coast
content: One of the wildest car, truck, and bike shows on the Gulf Coast featuring custom vehicles, lowriders, minitrucks, and sound systems. Enormous outdoor show with indoor coliseum section. Now in its 24th year.

title: 78th Annual Mississippi Deep Sea Fishing Rodeo
content: The nation's oldest fishing tournament, held since 1948. This national saltwater and freshwater fishing competition is open to all ages with free individual registration. Anglers fish anywhere in the Mississippi Sound or Gulf of Mexico and bring catches to the weigh station. Live entertainment, food, and family activities throughout the weekend.

title: Biloxi Shuckers Independence Day Fireworks
content: Celebrate America's 250th birthday at the ballpark vs Chattanooga Lookouts. Post-game fireworks extravaganza both nights. Military personnel receive discounted admission.

title: Fourth of July — America's 250th Birthday — Biloxi
content: 2026 marks America's Semiquincentennial (250th anniversary). Fireworks over the Sound launched from a barge near Deer Island, set to patriotic music. Best viewing from Biloxi Lighthouse to Point Cadet. Freedom Fest at Keesler AFB with music, games, and fireworks. Casino celebrations at Beau Rivage and other venues.

title: Fourth of July Celebration — Gulfport
content: Gulfport celebrates Independence Day with a waterfront celebration at Jones Park. Live music, food trucks, kids' activities, and a grand fireworks display over the harbor cap off the evening.

title: Blessing of the Fleet
content: Dating back to 1929, this tradition marks the beginning of shrimp season. Features a Shrimp Festival, arts and crafts, live entertainment, and a ceremonial wreath dropping honoring those lost at sea. Organized by St. Michael Catholic Church.
venue: Great Lawn (across from Harrah's Gulf Coast)

title: Back to School Bash — Harrison County
content: Harrison County families get ready for the new school year at the annual Back to School Bash. Free school supplies, backpacks, haircuts, health screenings, and community resources help families prepare.

title: Biloxi Shuckers Beach Chickens Night
content: Fan-favorite alternate identity night — the Biloxi Beach Chickens return with specialty jerseys. First 1,000 fans receive a Beach Chickens cooler bag.

title: Biloxi Shuckers Fan Appreciation Night
content: The Biloxi Shuckers celebrate their loyal fans with a special Fan Appreciation Night at Keesler Federal Park. Enjoy giveaways, prize drawings, post-game fireworks, and a thank-you from the team as the 2026 season winds down.

title: 45th Annual Biloxi Seafood Festival
content: The Biloxi Chamber of Commerce's biggest fundraiser and a celebration of Gulf Coast seafood heritage. Live entertainment, arts and crafts, gumbo cook-off, and the best seafood on the Mississippi Gulf Coast from local vendors and restaurants. Now in its 45th year.

title: 17th Annual Taste of Long Beach
content: Showcases local cuisine and beverage vendors from the Long Beach community. Live music, silent auction with items and experiences. Funds raised support small business grants and scholarships through the Long Beach Chamber of Commerce.
address: 301 Jeff Davis Ave., Long Beach, MS 39560

title: Cruisin' the Coast — 30th Anniversary
content: America's Largest Block Party celebrates its 30th anniversary. A week-long celebration of antique, classic, and hot rod vehicles with nearly 10,000 registered cars, 300+ vendors, and 30,000+ attendees. Rock 'n' roll entertainment, drag races, sock hops, swap meets, and cruising along the Gulf Coast.

title: D'Iberville Fall Festival
content: D'Iberville celebrates the fall season with a community festival at D'Iberville City Park. Enjoy hayrides, pumpkin patches, a corn maze, live music, food vendors, and family activities.

title: Halloween Events — Boo on the Rue & Trunk or Treats
content: Multiple Halloween community events across Harrison County including Boo on the Rue (family & pet-friendly outdoor Halloween party at Rue Magnolia Arts District), Trunk or Treat events at churches and parks, costume contests, and Kroctober Fest at the Kroc Center.
venue: Rue Magnolia Arts District & Multiple Locations

title: Veterans Day Ceremony — Biloxi
content: Biloxi honors all who have served with a Veterans Day ceremony at the Biloxi National Cemetery. The event features patriotic music, a rifle salute, guest speakers, and wreath-laying. Keesler Air Force Base personnel participate in the ceremony.

title: Scottish Highland Games & Celtic Music Festival
content: Two full days of Scottish culture, food, Celtic music, and Highland athletic competitions. Two stages of music and dance, jousting, axe throwing, Clan tents, artisan vendors, sheep-herding demonstrations, Parade of the Clans on Saturday, and Kirkin o' the Tartan on Sunday.

title: Thanksgiving Turkey Trot 5K — Biloxi
content: Start your Thanksgiving morning with the annual Turkey Trot 5K along Biloxi Beach. This fun run draws hundreds of runners and walkers of all ages. Costumes encouraged! Proceeds benefit local charities.

title: Gulfport Harbor Lights Winter Festival
content: Over 2 million lights spanning 40 acres in Jones Park and the Gulfport Harbor. Features Santa, dancing Christmas trees, walking trails, Ferris wheel, hot chocolate, and lighted displays. One of the largest Christmas light festivals in the South, presented by Island View Casino Resort and the City of Gulfport. Open nightly through December 31.

title: Lighting of the Biloxi Lighthouse
content: Biloxi officially kicks off the Christmas season with the ceremonial lighting of the historic Biloxi Lighthouse. A beloved holiday tradition.

title: Christmas in the Pass
content: Annual holiday celebration in Pass Christian with seasonal festivities, decorations, shopping, and community activities throughout downtown.

title: 40th Annual Christmas on the Water — Biloxi
content: Since 1986, this celebration marks the official opening of the holiday season for the Mississippi Gulf Coast. Features a decorated boat parade along the Mississippi Sound, fireworks display, holiday market with artists and vendors in downtown Biloxi, and Santa visit for children. Now in its 40th year.

title: Biloxi Christmas Parade
content: Biloxi rings in the holiday season with a spectacular Christmas parade through downtown and along the beach boulevard. Festive floats, marching bands, dance teams, and Santa himself make their way through the city spreading holiday cheer.

title: Gulfport Christmas Parade
content: The annual Gulfport Christmas Parade fills the streets with holiday magic. Beautifully decorated floats, school bands, community organizations, and Santa Claus parade through downtown Gulfport.

title: Long Beach Christmas Parade
content: Long Beach celebrates the holidays with its charming annual Christmas parade through town. Decorated floats, school groups, local businesses, and Santa Claus make their way down Jeff Davis Avenue.
address: Jeff Davis Ave., Long Beach, MS 39560

title: Pass Christian Christmas Parade
content: Pass Christian's quaint Christmas parade rolls through the scenic downtown area and along the harbor. A small-town parade capturing the magic of the holidays.

title: New Year's Eve Celebrations — Gulf Coast
content: The Mississippi Gulf Coast rings in 2027 at multiple venues with fireworks over the Gulf, live music, ball drops, and casino parties. Venues include Hard Rock Hotel & Casino, Margaritaville Resort Biloxi, Beau Rivage, Ground Zero Blues Club, and more. Long Beach hosts a Jeff Davis Ave ball drop at midnight. Gulfport features Countdown to Noon for kids at Lynn Meadows Discovery Center.
